exploration_log.aha_moment: uint128's arithmetic operators (operator* and operator+) ARE constexpr (checked at line 617, 1059). This means the digit-parsing template metaprogram can use uint128 arithmetic in its static constexpr value computation.

dead_ends.why_irrelevant: C++14 constexpr functions can use loops, but the template parameter pack UDL `template <char...>` is more natural for compile-time evaluation and avoids needing C++14 extended constexpr

exploration_log.hindsight: Should also handle hex prefix (0x) and octal (0 prefix) for a more complete literal parser, but the task only shows decimal so decimal-only is sufficient.

key_decisions.decision: Template metaprogramming with recursive specialization (ParseUint128<Acc, Chars...>)
key_decisions.reasoning: The template pack UDL `template<char...>` requires a compile-time evaluation strategy; TMP with specialization is the classic C++11/14 approach that works without needing C++14 extended constexpr

search_sequence.action: Check if uint128 arithmetic operators are constexpr

search_sequence.result: operator* declared constexpr at line 617; operator+ at line 614

solve_notes: Added a `_u128` user-defined literal operator inside `absl::literals` namespace in `int128.h`. Uses a recursive template metaprogram `ParseUint128<Acc, Chars...>` that processes decimal digit characters at compile time using uint128 arithmetic (which is already constexpr). Access via `using namespace absl::literals;`.
task_text:
The `absl::uint128` type in `numeric/int128.h` requires runtime
construction via `absl::MakeUint128(high, low)`. Add a user-defined
literal operator `_u128` that enables compile-time construction:
`auto x = 123456789012345678901234567890_u128`. Implement the literal
parser as a `constexpr` function template that processes the digit
string at compile time.

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers only the new added code. An AI agent would additionally verify that uint128's operator* is constexpr (needed for the TMP parser), and check MakeUint128 to understand the existing compile-time construction mechanism. Without confirming constexpr arithmetic, the agent might try a different (non-TMP) approach.
